sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Hellas Verona are struggling, sitting at the bottom of the Serie A table with no wins in their last 12 matches, including a recent 0-0 draw against Lecce. Parma, slightly better off at 17th with 8 points, have also faltered, managing just one win in their last 5 games, with a recent 2-2 draw against Milan showing some fight but defensive fragility.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Hellas Verona have the edge with a 3-2 victory over Parma in December 2024, though the most recent match in March 2025 ended in a 0-0 stalemate. Verona remain unbeaten in their last three head-to-heads, which could boost their confidence.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Verona’s attack is the weakest in the league with only 6 goals scored, and they struggle with possession (37.8%, worst in Serie A). Parma, with 7 goals, aren’t much better offensively, but their recent matches show a tendency for goals, with 4 of their last 5 exceeding 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Verona, Giovane’s creativity (3 assists, 5 big chances created) could be vital despite their scoring woes. Parma’s Adrián Bernabé (2 goals, 7.05 rating) and in-form Jacob Ondrejka (4 goals in last 5) pose threats if Verona’s defense lapses.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction (37% home win, 39% draw, 24% away win) and both teams’ poor form, a draw seems likely. Verona’s home advantage at Stadio Marcantonio Bentegodi and historical edge over Parma might be offset by Parma’s slightly better recent attacking output.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Parma leads Hellas Verona 1-0 at the Stadio Marcantonio Bentegodi. The visitors took the lead in the 18th minute through a headed goal by Mateo Pellegrino, capitalizing on a well-created opportunity. Hellas Verona, despite holding slightly more ball possession, failed to register any shots on target, while Parma converted one of their chances and even hit the woodwork. The home side showed resilience in aerial duels, dominating in that aspect, but struggled to create clear-cut chances. Parma, on the other hand, looked more clinical and managed to win a higher percentage of overall duels.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum
halftimeSections.text: The game started with Hellas Verona showing early intent, pushing forward with purpose in the opening minutes. However, Parma quickly shifted the momentum in their favor with a series of attacking spells, especially around the time of their goal. After taking the lead, the away side maintained pressure with consistent forays into the final third for a significant period. Towards the middle of the half, Verona regained some control, mounting a few promising attacks, but Parma responded with another wave of pressure. As the half drew to a close, Verona made a late push to level the score, showing determination to get back into the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key team insights
halftimeSections.text: Hellas Verona's performance in the first half was marked by a lack of cutting edge in the final third. Despite decent possession and numerous entries into the attacking zone, they couldn't translate their efforts into shots on target. Their aerial dominance offers a potential route for set-piece opportunities in the second half. Parma, meanwhile, demonstrated efficiency with limited possession, creating a big chance that led to their goal and missing another. Their ability to win duels has been crucial in maintaining control, though their weakness in aerial battles could be exploited by Verona.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Standout players
halftimeSections.text: For Hellas Verona, Jean-Daniel Akpa Akpro stood out with a strong display in midfield, contributing defensively and showing attacking intent. Gift Orban also showed glimpses of threat upfront, though he couldn't find the target. On Parma's side, Mateo Pellegrino was the difference-maker with his well-taken goal, proving to be a constant menace. Lautaro Valenti was impressive at the back, making key clearances and winning crucial duels to keep Verona at bay.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Hellas Verona come out with renewed energy as they chase an equalizer. Given their possession advantage and aerial strength, they may focus on set-pieces or crosses to unlock Parma's defense. Parma, on the other hand, will aim to protect their lead and could look to exploit counter-attacking opportunities, especially if Verona pushes forward. Both teams have shown enough in the first half to suggest that the game remains open, with chances likely to emerge as Verona presses for a goal.
